meta.title: AI Corporate Market Concentration by 2028
meta.question: By 2028-Q4, do the top-3 commercial generative-AI API providers hold ≥60% combined revenue share of the global commercial GenAI API market, as reported by at least one major market-research firm (Gartner, IDC, Bloomberg Intelligence, or equivalent)?
meta.resolution: Resolved H1 (hyper-concentrated) if best available market-research data shows top-3 providers at ≥75% combined revenue share. H2 (concentrated) if 60-74%. H3 (fragmented) if 40-59%. H4 (commoditized) if <40%. Primary source: Gartner/IDC annual AI market report or Bloomberg Intelligence published before 2029-03-01. Fallback: consensus of ≥3 analyst firms. resolutionDate: 2028-12-31.

tagConfig._docs: EARNINGS/BENCHMARK/SURVEY favor H1/H2 (concentration) or H3/H4 (fragmentation) per direction. REGULATORY always favors H3/H4.

H1.label: Hyper-concentrated: top-3 hold ≥75% GenAI API revenue share
H1.description: OpenAI/Google/Anthropic maintain dominant moat. Open-weights models fail commercially. Infrastructure lock-in (contracts, fine-tuning, compliance) sustains oligopoly. Malone's local-models thesis fails at commercial scale.

H2.label: Concentrated oligopoly: top-3 hold 60-74% GenAI API revenue share
H2.description: Top-3 remain dominant but face meaningful competition from open-weights commercial deployments (Meta Llama, Mistral, Cohere) and regional providers. Significant moat persists via safety certs, RLHF quality, and enterprise SLAs.

H3.label: Fragmented market: top-3 hold 40-59% GenAI API revenue share
H3.description: Open-weights commercial adoption, regional providers, and enterprise self-hosting erode top-3 share. Llama commercial licensing, Mistral B2B, and self-hosted deployments collectively capture >40% of enterprise spend.

H4.label: Commoditized: top-3 hold <40% GenAI API revenue share
H4.description: Open-weights achieve commercial parity — capability gap closes, inference costs equalize, enterprises self-host at scale. Top-3 moat collapses. Validates Malone's claim that local models are too accessible for a durable moat.

posteriorHistory.note: Initial priors. OpenAI/Google/Anthropic estimated at >80% GenAI API share Q1 2026 (no authoritative public figure — analyst consensus). Concentrated-camp prior (H1+H2=60%) reflects current dominance and structural advantages (enterprise contracts, safety certs, RLHF quality). Fragmentation-camp (H3+H4=40%) reflects Meta Llama commercial trajectory, open-weights benchmark convergence trend, and historical pattern of infrastructure commoditization within 3-5 years of tech waves. H2 > H1 because full 75%+ lock-in requires no meaningful open-weights commercial revenue — less likely given Meta's strategic commitment to Llama. Prior informed by Bsky thread (proptermalone/lastnpcalex debate, 2026-04-22) — moat debate sourced from social media RHETORIC, no fired indicators.

Directional concentration signal: top-3 enterprise LLM share reported ~88%, consistent with H1/H2. Not Gartner/IDC annual — t1_top3_80pct_share_confirmed NOT FIRED. Likelihoods tilt toward concentration but conservative (analyst synthesis, not audited).

tier1_critical.desc: Gartner, IDC, or Bloomberg Intelligence publishes annual GenAI API market report showing top-3 providers at ≥80% combined revenue share in any calendar year 2026-2028. Observable via published market research reports.

An open-weights AI provider (Meta Llama API, Mistral, Cohere, or equivalent) reports ≥$5B annual GenAI API revenue in any annual earnings or Bloomberg-verified report before 2028-12-31. Observable via SEC filings or earnings calls.

tier2_strong.desc: Gartner or State of AI annual survey confirms ≥30% of Fortune 500 enterprise AI deployments run primarily on open-weights models without top-3 API fees.
OpenAI discloses ≥$60B annual revenue with ≥65% attributed to GenAI API/enterprise products (per IPO S-1, annual report, or Bloomberg verified figure).
EU Digital Markets Act enforcement or US DOJ/FTC filing formally classifies AI API market as unlawfully concentrated or designates a major AI provider as 'gatekeeper' with structural remedy orders.

At least 2 independent enterprise GenAI spending surveys (Menlo Ventures, Ramp AI Index, Similarweb, a16z State of AI, Stack Overflow Developer Survey, or equivalent) report top-3 GenAI providers at >=75% combined enterprise share for >=2 consecutive quarters before 2028-12-31. Observable via published survey reports.

tier3_suggestive.desc: Best open-weights model scores within 10% of best closed frontier model on ≥3 major benchmarks (HELM, LMSYS Chatbot Arena ELO, MMLU-Pro) for ≥2 consecutive quarters.
Stack Overflow Developer Survey or State of AI shows ≥40% of enterprises regularly use ≥3 different AI providers (low lock-in signal).

Top-4 frontier AI labs (by ARR or market cap) capture >=60% of global AI venture funding for >=2 consecutive quarters before 2028-12-31, per PitchBook, CB Insights, or Crunchbase quarterly reports. Observable via published VC funding aggregates.

anti_indicators.desc: Open-weights provider reports >$5B annual commercial revenue — contradicts hyper-concentrated moat.
Major research firm publishes top-3 combined revenue share below 50% — contradicts oligopoly.
Top-3 provider acquires leading open-weights competitor (Meta AI, Mistral, Cohere, or equivalent with >$3B valuation) — market reconsolidates.
Top-3 maintain ≥70% combined share through 2027 with no regulatory challenge and no open-weights provider exceeding $1B commercial revenue — H4 commoditization implausible.

An open-weights model holds #1 position on LMSYS Chatbot Arena ELO leaderboard or HELM aggregate score for >=1 calendar quarter before 2028-12-31. Capability-parity moat-erosion signal independent of commercial revenue thresholds.

actorModel.description: Commercial AI market concentration driven by provider pricing, open-source release strategy, hyperscaler lock-in, and regulatory intervention.

openai.name: OpenAI
openai.role: Current market leader — largest GenAI API revenue and developer mindshare
openai.decisionStyle: Rational/commercial. Capped-profit structure creates IPO pressure. Balance revenue growth vs. developer adoption.
overrides: Open-weights parity threat could force price cuts or open-sourcing older models

google_deepmind.name: Google DeepMind / Gemini
google_deepmind.role: Second-largest — defends via GCP infrastructure lock-in and enterprise integration
google_deepmind.decisionStyle: Institutional/defensive. TPU access, GCP bundling, Workspace integration as moat.
biases: Internal competition between Search and Cloud AI divisions
filters: DOJ antitrust scrutiny (existing search monopoly case)
overrides: OpenAI dominance threat could force aggressive price undercutting

meta_ai.name: Meta AI (Llama)
meta_ai.role: Key swing actor — open Llama release undermines moat for all parties including Meta
meta_ai.decisionStyle: Strategic openness. Meta benefits from preventing OpenAI/Google monopoly (lowers own inference costs). Zuckerberg: 'open-source is right AND good for Meta'.
biases: Reluctance to build API business competing with own social AI
overrides: Revenue pressure could shift Llama from open to commercial API model

regulators.name: EU/US Regulatory Bodies (DMA, FTC, DOJ)
regulators.decisionStyle: Institutional/precedent-bound. EU DMA already designates gatekeepers; AI-specific provisions likely 2026-2028.
biases: Slow to act on novel tech; prefer ex-ante frameworks
overrides: Consumer harm evidence or China AI threat could accelerate intervention

methodology: ACTIONS OVER RHETORIC — only verified revenue figures and published reports move posteriors
WATCH META — Llama commercial trajectory is the single most important swing variable for H3/H4
DON'T FRONT-RUN regulatory filings; wait for formal designations not press releases
TAG STATEMENTS — CEO open-source pledges = RHETORIC until revenue confirms
